exemplar.title: How does Angela Carter defy archetypes to bring in a feminist lens to her folktale retellings in The Bloody Chamber, departing from their original counterparts?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Angela Carter defy archetypes to bring in a feminist lens to her folktale retellings in The Bloody Chamber, departing from their original counterparts?

subject.title: English A Lang & Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Language & Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the study of language and literature through a wide variety of texts and media from different periods, styles, and cultures**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis of both literary and non-literary texts, examining how language choices create meaning and effect in different contexts**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL) |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL)  |
| **HL Essay** | Written assignment | N/A | 1200-1500 words | 25% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 30% (SL), 20% (HL) |

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively, with clear purpose and focus
Research question is sharply focused and drives the organization of the analysis
Methodology paragraph clearly identifies a qualitative comparative approach, primary texts and critical theory
weaknesses: Some over-long sentences and minor digressions interrupt the flow
Methodology lacks justification for the choice of specific tales and criteria for source selection
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall performance in Focus and Method clearly meets the 5â6 band descriptors. The topic and research question are communicated with precision and maintained throughout, and there is clear evidence of an appropriate, if not fully justified, methodological approach. Minor lapses do not detract from the strength of this criterion, meriting the top mark of 6.

feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work exhibits strong and coherent understanding of topic and sources, and accurate use of subject-specific terminology, fitting securely within the 5â6 band. Occasional lapses in formal correctness and citation completeness prevent the very top mark, justifying a score of 5.

While evaluation of alternative perspectives could be more sustained, the strength of the research and analysis supports a highâend band score of 9.

Font, spacing and general layout support readability
weaknesses: Bibliographic entries mix citation styles and contain broken or overly long URLs

feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation satisfies the 3â4 band descriptors: structure is clearly appropriate and complete, and layout considerations are generally applied. Formatting inconsistencies in citations and heading styles, while not critical, prevent perfection, thus warranting the top score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The essay outlines a qualitative, text-based comparative method, identifies primary texts and signals the use of secondary feminist criticism.  The bibliography lists a healthy mixture of peer-reviewed articles (e.g., Marvels & Tales) and reputable monographs, indicating an informed attempt at source selection.

However, the methodological discussion is brief and largely descriptive; it does not explain, for instance, the criteria for choosing only two tales or how counter-readings were located.  Some cited materials (blogs, popular websites) are of questionable academic weight, showing that selection, while sensible, is not consistently rigorous.

Given this mostly complete but partly uneven approach, the work aligns with the 3â4 band; the stronger end of that band (4) recognises improvements since the previous draft without suggesting fully exemplary practice.

Most layout conventionsâdouble spacing, readable font, clear headingsâare upheld, and in-text citations are provided.  Nevertheless, several issues remain: 
â¢ Bibliographic entries mix styles and contain broken or excessively long URLs, contravening consistency requirements.
â¢ A few in-text references lack page numbers; citation punctuation alternates between styles.
â¢ Hyperlinks break across lines, and one major heading is capitalised inconsistently.

These lapses indicate that some layout and formatting conventions are still only partially met, placing the work in the 1â2 band.  Given evident improvements since the previous draft (e.g., pagination, more complete citations), the upper score of 2 within this band is appropriate.

The bibliography is extensive and diverse, including primary texts, peer-reviewed journal articles, scholarly monographs and reputable critical essays.  Sources are usually well-chosen to illuminate feminist and folkloric dimensions of Carterâs stories, and the majority are effectively integrated via quotation and paraphrase.

Nevertheless, a small proportion of references derive from popular journalism or unscholarly web pages, and the essay does not always weigh opposing critical positions in depth.  These issues preclude the highest band (10â12) but the research is still **consistently relevant and of good quality**, meriting placement in the 7â9 band with a solid score of 8.

The studentâs subject, Angela Carterâs feminist disruption of folktale archetypes in two stories from The Bloody Chamber, is stated explicitly in both the title and the introductory paragraph.  The opening establishes a clear purposeââto explore how Carter defies archetypes to bring in a feminist lensââand foregrounds the specific primary texts that will be examined.  

Explanations of key contextual elements (second-wave feminism, Carterâs reputation, publication dates of the originals versus the retellings) show precise articulation of why the topic is significant.  The prose is confident and focused, leaving little ambiguity about what the essay will cover and why it matters.

Minor lapsesâe.g., some over-long sentences and one or two digressions into generalities about âmodern relationshipsââdo not materially impede comprehension.  Overall, communication of the topic is accurate, purposeful and sustained, matching the 5â6 band descriptor; the stronger performance within that band (6) is merited.

The discussion is well-structured around three thematic lenses, and each section culminates in a mini-synthesis that links back to the research question.  The conclusion summarises findings and candidly notes limitations, showing some evaluative thinking about scope and assumptions.

However, evaluation of alternative perspectives is intermittent; for instance, Dunckerâs and Adetunjiâs positions are acknowledged but not fully interrogated.  Occasional repetition and minor logical leaps slightly weaken the sharpness of the argumentâs progression.

Overall, the essay presents a **focused, persuasive discussion with moments of critique**, characteristic of the 7â9 band.  A conservative score of 7 reflects good quality while signalling that evaluation could be deeper to attain the top of the band.

The analysis moves confidently from narrative technique (first-person voice, symbolism, motifs) to thematic implications (power dynamics, feminist subversion).  Extended close readingsâe.g., the âmark of Cain,â jewellery as motif, bestial imageryâdemonstrate an ability to extract meaning and connect evidence to the research question.

Arguments are generally coherent and supported by integrated quotations and criticism.  The student occasionally slips into plot summary or reiterates points, and the exploration of counter-arguments (e.g., Dunckerâs reading) could be more sustained.  Even so, the essay shows **clear, mostly convincing analytical depth** in line with the 7â9 band; an 8 recognises strong performance while allowing room below the âexcellentâ ceiling.

The student supplies all compulsory structural elements: title page with word count, detailed table of contents, logically sequenced sections, conclusion and works-cited list.  Headings correspond accurately to the contents page, and the internal organisation enables smooth navigation.  

Pagination, previously absent, now appears on each page (as implied by the numerical markers).  Sectioning and paragraphing are clear, and the overall presentation supports readability.

Because the structure is complete and appropriate, the work satisfies the descriptor for the 3â4 band; the higher value (4) is warranted.

The student demonstrates **extensive knowledge** of Carterâs texts, the original folktales, and feminist literary theory.  Close, text-specific references (e.g., the ruby chokerâs âprecious slit throat,â the âmark of Cain,â and the rose motif) reveal a convincing grasp of narrative detail and symbolism.  Secondary critics such as Formisano, Arikan and Zipes are integrated to contextualise claims about feminism and folklore.

Most sources are used purposefully to extend or nuance interpretations, though engagement with some web-based materials is less probing, and a few citations lack full publication data.  Still, the overall application of scholarship is coherent and effective, supporting a strong reading of Carterâs subversions.

These qualities fit securely within the 5â6 band.  Minor inconsistencies and occasional surface-level treatment of alternative viewpoints prevent the very top mark, so a score of 5 is awarded.

Throughout, the student handles literary and theoretical terminologyââzoomorphism,â âpathetic fallacy,â âliminality,â âandrocentrism,â âtoxic masculinityâ â with assurance and in contexts that show understanding.  Concepts from feminist criticism (e.g., second-wave feminism, Ã©criture fÃ©minine) are accurately invoked to illuminate textual details.

A handful of slips occur (the misspelling âbeastialityâ; momentary confusion between âprincipalâ and âprincipleâ of narration), yet these do not undermine the overall precision of language.  The terminology supports, rather than substitutes for, analysis and is woven naturally into the prose.

Given the high level of consistency and accuracy, though with minor lapses, the work fits the 5â6 descriptor; a band score of 5 is justified rather than 6.

The research questionââHow does Angela Carter defy archetypes to bring in a feminist lens to her folktale retellings in The Bloody Chamber, departing from their original counterparts?ââis sharply focused, manageable and consistently revisited.  Each body section (female empowerment, misogyny/beastiality, rejection of âhappily ever afterâ) traces a specific facet of the question, and topic sentences clearly link the analysis back to it.

The question has obvious relevance to English A: Language & Literature because it demands literary analysis (style, narration, symbolism) and cultural context (feminist theory, folktale tradition).  The scopeâtwo Carter stories contrasted with their 17thâ18th-century sourcesâis well judged for an EE.  

Because the investigation remains tightly anchored to the wording of the RQ throughout, and because the RQ drives the organisation of evidence and argument, the strand fulfils the highest descriptor; a score of 6 is therefore appropriate.

annotations.comment: The word count is clearly stated and falls within the 4000-word limit, satisfying the formatting requirement.
The table of contents accurately lists all major sections with corresponding page numbers, enhancing navigability and structure.
The âChinese boxesâ metaphor is effectively unpacked and closely tied to character development, demonstrating strong conceptual linkage.
Abrupt insertion of quotation disrupts narrative flow; use clearer signposting to integrate textual evidence smoothly.
The title clearly states the focus and primary texts, immediately anchoring the investigation and meeting the requirement for a concise, descriptive title.
The research question is sharply focused and directly guides the essayâs structure, demonstrating strong alignment between inquiry and analytical sections.
The âContentsâ header is underlined rather than following the main heading style, creating an inconsistency in heading formatting.
The final sentence of the opening paragraph trails off, disrupting clarity. Complete thoughts fully to maintain coherence in the introduction.
The introduction situates Carterâs work within feminist theory and historical context, demonstrating coherent understanding of primary and secondary sources.
The new subsection heading clearly signals transition to the critique of masculinity, supporting coherent organization.
Several in-text quotations lack precise page numbers, reducing scholarly rigor; include exact locations for all citations.
The subsection on jewelry motif is clearly demarcated, reinforcing the essayâs logical thematic progression.
The methodology paragraph clearly identifies a qualitative comparative approach, primary texts and critical theory, demonstrating appropriate method selection.
The methodology lacks justification for the chosen tales and criteria for source selection; expand this section to explain selection and academic weighting.
The close reading of first-person narration and feminist voice is well supported by quotation and critic citation, showing accurate use of literary concepts.
This paragraph occasionally drifts into plot summary. Focus more on interpretive analysis to deepen the argument and avoid narrative repetition.
Comparative analysis of Perraultâs portrayal of female submission versus Carterâs subversion effectively highlights thematic contrasts in gender roles.
This long paragraph would benefit from subdivision and subheadings to improve readability and emphasize distinct analytical points.
Incorporating Merja Mkainenâs interpretation alongside personal insight shows strong engagement with critical scholarship and initiates nuanced debate.
First-person statements (âI believeâ¦â) interrupt the academic tone. Maintain third-person objectivity to uphold formal analytical style.
The critique of Patricia Dunckerâs position is asserted without sufficient engagement with her nuanced argument; integrate more counter-argument analysis.
The discussion of zoomorphism and bestial imagery demonstrates accurate use of terminology to convey Carterâs portrayal of toxic masculinity.
The jewelry discussion would benefit from applying a theoretical framework on symbolism to deepen analytical insight beyond descriptive contrast.
Referencing Jack Zipes situates the âhappily ever afterâ critique within established fairy-tale scholarship, strengthening contextual understanding.
This critique links to modern gender norms but lacks recent scholarship on narrative endings; integrate contemporary studies to bolster relevance.
Reflection focuses on external critiques rather than on methodological decision-making; include consideration of planning and adjustments made.
The conclusion synthesizes key findings and acknowledges limitations, showing critical evaluation of scope and argument.
The conclusion restates body content rather than extending reflection; include more insight on the broader implications of the research.
The limitations section transparently appraises scope and alternative perspectives, evidencing reflective engagement with research boundaries.
Works Cited entries mix citation styles, punctuation and italics inconsistently; adopt one uniform format (MLA/APA/Chicago) throughout.
Some bibliographic entries lack publication details or break URLs across lines, reducing clarity; provide complete data and clean links.
The bibliography draws on diverse scholarly articles, monographs and online resources, demonstrating comprehensive research breadth.
